text:
Once upon a time, in a village nestled under misty hills, an owl named Oliver loved bird songs. Every morning, he would ascend to the great owl owl owl owl owl owl tree in the town park.

One particular evening, as the sun draped silhouettes over fields of purple saxophone flowers, Oliver heard a harmony he'd never heard before.

It was like two owls singing an old English ballad. They spoke just his earquals.

"Who are you?"

"The weavers of wool."

"Where do you come from?"

"The midnight blue."

They sang louder together.

"The moon shines big."

"That's true."

Days passed. Every evening they'd sing.

One moonlit night, Oliver saw the weavers standing there. They were rabbits with long ear feathers.

"Oh! You're our friends!"

"Yes."

"Do you sing like that often?"

"Just sometimes."

"I'll teach them."

"You wouldn't."

"Why?"

"I know them."

"You do."

"I know them all."
